en.extract:
The mountain sooty boubou (Laniarius poensis), western boubou or mountain boubou, is a species of bird in the family Malaconotidae.


Taxonomy
It was formerly considered conspecific with the Albertine sooty boubou (Laniarius holomelas), with Willard's sooty boubou (Laniarius willardi), and with Fülleborn's sooty boubou (Laniarius fuelleborni).
Two subspecies are currently recognized: nominate L. p. poensis, which is endemic to Bioko; and L. p. camerunensis which occurs on mainland Africa.


Distribution and habitat
It is found in the Cameroon line from Bioko, Equatorial Guinea north to Mount Oku, Cameroon and the Obudu Plateau, Nigeria. In the Cameroon highlands, it is found in montane forests, usually above 600 m in elevation. On Mount Cameroon, this species can occur at extremely low elevations on the windward slopes near the coast, with records from as low as c. 520 m above sea level. On both Bioko and Mt. Cameroon, this taxon is restricted to montane forests.

The '''mountain sooty boubou''' ('''''Laniarius poensis'''''), '''western boubou''' or '''mountain boubou''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Malaconotidae]].

==Taxonomy==

It was formerly considered [[conspecific]] with the [[Albertine sooty boubou]] (''Laniarius holomelas''), with [[Willard's sooty boubou]] (''Laniarius willardi''), and with [[Fülleborn's boubou|Fülleborn's sooty boubou]] (''Laniarius fuelleborni'').<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | year=2019 | title=Batises, woodshrikes, bushshrikes, vangas | work=World Bird List Version 9.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/batises/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=16 January 2019 }}</ref>

Two subspecies are currently recognized: nominate ''L. p. poensis'', which is [[Endemism|endemic]] to Bioko; and ''L. p. camerunensis'' which occurs on mainland Africa.<ref name=bow>{{cite journal |last1=Fry |first1=Hilary |last2=Kirwan |first2=Guy M. |editor-first1=Shawn M. |editor-first2=Brooke K. |editor-first3=Paul G. |editor-first4=Thomas S. |editor-last1=Billerman |editor-last2=Keeney |editor-last3=Rodewald |editor-last4=Schulenberg |title=Western Boubou (Laniarius poensis) |journal=Birds of the World |date=4 March 2020 |doi=10.2173/bow.mosbou1.01 |publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology |location=Ithaca, New York, USA|s2cid=242408833 }}</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==

It is found in the [[Cameroon line]] from [[Bioko]], [[Equatorial Guinea]] north to [[Mount Oku]], [[Cameroon]] and the [[Obudu Plateau]], [[Nigeria]].<ref name="bow" /> In the [[Western High Plateau|Cameroon highlands]], it is found in [[Cameroonian Highlands forests|montane forests]], usually above 600 m in elevation.<ref name="bow" /> On [[Mount Cameroon]], this species can occur at extremely low elevations on the [[Windward and leeward|windward]] slopes near the coast, with records from as low as c. 520 m above sea level.<ref name="serle_elev">{{cite journal |last1=Serle |first1=William |title=The lower altitudinal limit of the montane forest birds of the Cameroon Mountain, West Africa |journal=Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club |date=1964 |volume=84 |pages=87–91}}</ref> On both Bioko and Mt. Cameroon, this taxon is restricted to [[Mount Cameroon and Bioko montane forests|montane forests]].
